multi_perspective_review.note: Bare planner abstains reasoned from 5 perspectives. Findings + dropped_flags kept verbatim (both empty: the deterministic pass emitted no flags). Recomputable numbers (c6 ratio, c9 pH/Rayleigh, c13 R^2<->r, c17/c18 geometry) were recomputed and found clean -> CONFIRMED-CORRECT with an auditable advisory:claims-auditor drop-note; genuine model-judgement concerns -> advisory:<perspective>; significance/novelty -> subjective; only c16 (robustness vs unavailable SI Fig S8, no evidence record) remains abstained.

routed_to_human.note: Central result: an outward IEF of ~1.0-1.2 V/Aa along the Willard-Chandler surface normal, ~5-6x larger than prior computational estimates (~0.2 V/Aa, c5). In chemistry terms the magnitude is physically large but not impossible for a DFT-derived local field at a sharp instantaneous interface; the WC-normal projection and the choice of where the 'interface' sits strongly affect the magnitude. CONFIRM: independent recompute of the field on the released DPMD/DFT configurations, and a sensitivity check of the value to the interface (WC) definition and to the averaging volume. No numeric verifier regenerates a DFT field distribution from the paper text alone.
routed_to_human.quote: find an outward-oriented field of ∼ 1.0–1.2 V/˚A along the intrinsic surface normal

routed_to_human.note: Mechanistic claim that IEF magnitude scales LINEARLY with average H-bonds per interfacial molecule, resting on the Fig 2b fit (R^2=0.994). The internal fit statistic is self-consistent (see c13), but 'linear scaling' is generalized from a handful of system sizes spanning a narrow H-bond range; linearity over that window does not establish a globally linear law. CONFIRM: the per-system (H-bond, IEF) table plus residuals across the full curvature series. Concern is a possible over-generalization of a narrow-range correlation, not a numeric error.
routed_to_human.quote:
its magnitude scales linearly with the average number of hydrogen bonds per
interfacial molecule

routed_to_human.note: The ~1e-5 change in IEF between 3 and 40 um droplets is an EXTRAPOLATION ~3 orders of magnitude beyond the simulated size range (44-68 Aa = 0.0044-0.0068 um) using a fitted curvature dependence (c15). The simulated trend may be sound, but the headline 'effectively curvature-independent up to 40 um' rests on the assumed extrapolation functional form, not on data at those sizes. CONFIRM: the curvature fit, its uncertainty band, and a stated justification for the 1/R extrapolation law to micron scale. Not recomputable from the released nanoscale data.
routed_to_human.quote:
which changes by a factor
of only ∼ 10−5 between 3 and 40µm-sized droplets

routed_to_human.note: Qualitative spatial claim that the IEF is localized to the interface and decays within a few Aa. Consistent with the field definition and with the vapor1/vapor2 profile (c11). Plausible and field-standard for an interfacial field, but 'a few Aa' and 'rapidly vanishes' are imprecise; the apparent decay length depends on the chosen bin width and interface reference. CONFIRM: the field-vs-distance profile with bin sizes and the decay length read off it.
routed_to_human.quote:
the IEF is localized
inside the interfacial region and rapidly vanishes within a few ˚A

routed_to_human.note: This ~0.2 V/Aa value is attributed to prior literature (refs 6,9,11-13), not produced by this work; it anchors the 5-6x novelty claim (c6). T5 external-literature check: whether 0.2 V/Aa faithfully represents those sources (same field definition, same WC-normal projection, same averaging) cannot be settled from this paper. A definitional mismatch (e.g. macroscopic vs instantaneous-interface field) would deflate the 5-6x comparison. CONFIRM: read the cited sources and match field definitions.
routed_to_human.quote: that the air-water boundary may sustain electric fields on the order of ∼ 0.2 V/˚A

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed: the stated '5-6 times larger' is arithmetically exact for the reported numbers (1.0/0.2 = 5.0 and 1.2/0.2 = 6.0), so the ratio itself is CONFIRMED-CORRECT; no numeric verifier currently emits this T2 ratio check. Residual (non-numeric) concern: the comparison is only meaningful if this work's IEF and the ~0.2 V/Aa prior estimate use the same field definition (see c5). The number is not over-reaching; the like-for-like comparability is what a human should confirm.
routed_to_human.quote:
i.e., about 5-6 times larger than previous computational
estimates.

routed_to_human.note: Simulation protocol (NVT, 300 K, CSVR thermostat, damping 0.04 ps) is standard and internally consistent. METHODOLOGIST concern: 'at least 1 ns' of DPMD per system is on the short side for converging interfacial field and H-bond statistics, especially for the larger droplets; sampling adequacy bears on the precision of c1/c2/c14. CONFIRM: block-averaged error bars or a convergence-vs-time plot for the IEF. Parameter values themselves are not in dispute.
routed_to_human.quote:
Each DPMD simulation was
carried out in the NVT canonical ensemble for at least 1 ns. The temperature was kept fixed
at 300 K via the canonical-sampling-velocity-rescaling (CSVR) thermostat 55 with a damping
factor of 0.04 ps.

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed: N=500 randomly chosen configurations re-sampled at PBE+D3 is a transcribed methods parameter with no internal arithmetic to violate; CONFIRMED-CORRECT on its face. No numeric verifier covers it yet. Minor methodological footnote (for a human, not a flag): whether 500 single-point DFT frames suffice for the reported distribution widths is a sampling-adequacy question tied to c7, addressable via the released configuration set.
routed_to_human.quote:
500 randomly chosen configurations were extracted from all DPMD trajectories and re-
sampled via ab initio calculations at the PBE+D3

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed and internally cross-checked: the four charge states [-5e,-1e,+1e,+5e] map to four pH values, and the conjugate pairs sum to ~14 (13.74+0.26=14.00; 13.0+0.96=13.96), consistent with water's Kw; the 13% and 66% Rayleigh-limit fractions track the 5x charge ratio (13x5=65 ~ 66). All CONFIRMED-CORRECT; no numeric verifier emits this pH/Rayleigh consistency check yet. The 'approximately correspond to net pH' mapping (excess charge -> bulk-equivalent pH for a finite nanodroplet) is a modeling convention a human may wish to note, but it is not an arithmetic error.
routed_to_human.quote:
charge states corresponding to −5e
−, −e−, +e−, and +5e−, hence probing regimes of 13%
and 66% of the Rayleigh limit for Coulomb fission. These charge states approximately cor-
respond to net pH values of 13.74, 13.0, 0.96, and 0.26, respectively.

routed_to_human.note: Claim of LINEAR increase of IEF with total excess charge, resting on the Fig 3a linear fit over only four charge states (-5e,-1e,+1e,+5e). Four points can be fit by a line almost regardless of the true functional form; linearity is asserted, not demonstrated against a non-linear alternative. CONFIRM: the four (charge, IEF) values with uncertainties and the fit residuals. Possible over-reach from sparse sampling, not a numeric mistake.
routed_to_human.quote: that the IEF linearly increases with the total excess charge

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed: vapor1 ~0.3 V/Aa and vapor2 vanishing-within-uncertainty are transcribed region-averaged values supporting the decay claim (c4); on their face CONFIRMED-CORRECT with no numeric verifier covering them. Residual concern for a human: the values depend on how the vapor1/vapor2 region boundaries are drawn relative to the interface, which is a binning choice rather than a computed quantity. CONFIRM via the field-vs-distance profile with region definitions.
routed_to_human.quote:
In the vapor1 region the typical field values drop to smaller
values of approximately ∼ 0.3 V/˚A, while in the vapor2 region the field vanishes within
the statistical uncertainty.

routed_to_human.note: The interfacial field DISTRIBUTION peaks at 1.2-1.4 V/Aa, while the headline reported field (c1) is 1.0-1.2 V/Aa. These are different statistics (modal peak of a broad distribution vs the reported representative value), so they need not coincide, but the ~0.2 V/Aa offset and the choice of which statistic to headline should be reconciled so readers don't conflate them. CONFIRM: the distribution with its peak and mean/median marked. Not a numeric error; a presentation/consistency point.
routed_to_human.quote:
The corresponding distributions peak at approximately
1.2–1.4 V/˚A

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed and recomputed: for a simple linear fit R^2 = r^2, and (-0.997)^2 = 0.994009, which rounds to the reported R^2=0.994; equivalently sqrt(0.994)=0.99700 rounds to |r|=0.997. The reported R^2 and Pearson r are fully internally CONSISTENT -> CONFIRMED-CORRECT. No numeric verifier currently emits this R^2<->r coherence check (a clean T0 synthesis candidate, DESIGN S8/WS-F). Drop recorded here so the resolution is auditable rather than silent.

Reviewed: mean dipole tilt alpha ~96 deg (droplet) / ~94 deg (slab) relative to the outward normal gives cos(alpha)<0, consistent with the stated net-outward field direction; the direction logic is self-consistent. Both angles sit only 4-6 deg past 90 deg, i.e. a weak average orientational preference, so the qualitative 'net outward' conclusion is direction-correct but rests on a small mean asymmetry whose statistical significance depends on the (short, c7) sampling. CONFIRM: the alpha distribution width / standard error. Not a numeric error.
routed_to_human.quote:
leading to an
average α ∼ 96◦in the droplet (α ∼ 94◦in the slab), implies a net electric field directed
outward from the liquid toward the vapor.

routed_to_human.note: Quantitative restatement of the curvature extrapolation (c3): a ~1e-5 V/Aa variation, '5 orders of magnitude smaller than the field.' This depends entirely on the fitted curvature law extended from ~5 nm droplets to 3-40 um (a >1000x reach). The order-of-magnitude conclusion is only as good as the assumed 1/R-type form and its uncertainty. CONFIRM: the fit, its error band, and the physical justification for extrapolating it three decades in size. Not recomputable from the released data.
routed_to_human.quote:
reveals variations on the order of 10−5 V/˚A, i.e., five orders of magnitude smaller than
the field itself.

routed_to_human.note: Functional-robustness claim: PBE+D3, SCAN and r2SCAN yield 'essentially indistinguishable' IEF distributions. This is the key defense that the surprisingly large field is not a GGA/PBE artifact, but the supporting evidence (Fig S8) is in the unavailable SI and no evidence record backs the claim, so it cannot be assessed here. CONFIRM: obtain Fig S8 and quantify 'indistinguishable' (distribution overlap / shift in the peak field). Flagged advisory; also retained in abstained as genuinely unassessable pending the SI.
routed_to_human.quote: which yield essentially indistinguishable IEF distributions (see Fig. S8 of the SI)

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed: the 256/512-molecule slab box edges (a=b=19.641, c=45.000 Aa; a=b=24.830, c=60.000 Aa) are transcribed methods values with no internal arithmetic to violate -> CONFIRMED-CORRECT. No numeric verifier covers it yet. (A density cross-check against the molecule count would require the released coordinates.) Drop recorded for auditability.
routed_to_human.quote:
samples composed of 256 and 512 H2O molecules were considered with box edges of
a = b = 19.641, c = 45.000 ˚A and a = b = 24.830, c = 60.000 ˚A, respectively

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed and sanity-checked: 7 droplet sizes [256..1024] pair 1:1 with 7 box edges [44..68 Aa]; both lists are monotonically increasing, and the bulk-density-equivalent droplet diameters (~24-39 Aa) all fit inside their cubic boxes with vacuum padding, so the geometry is self-consistent -> CONFIRMED-CORRECT. No numeric verifier emits this list-coherence/geometry check yet. Drop recorded for auditability.
routed_to_human.quote:
nanodroplets composed of 256, 384, 512, 640, 768, 896, and 1024 H2O molecules were simu-
lated in cubic boxes with edges of 44, 48, 60, 63, 65, 67, and 68 ˚A, respectively

routed_to_human.note: Whether a 5-6x-larger interfacial field that is near-independent of curvature and pH is an important advance for electrospray/microdroplet chemistry and interfacial reactivity is a significance/novelty judgement (T8) - surfaced, not scored. Routed to a domain human.

evidence.quote: which yield essentially indistinguishable IEF distributions (see Fig. S8 of the SI)

location.quote: which yield essentially indistinguishable IEF distributions (see Fig. S8 of the SI)
